name: Haber Process (Ammonia Synthesis)
description: Reversible industrial synthesis of ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen gas.

name: Angular Momentum
description: Angular momentum of a rigid body as the product of its moment of inertia and angular velocity.

name: Area of a Circle
description: Area enclosed by a circle of radius r.

name: Sum of an Arithmetic Series
description: Sum of the first n positive integers; the simplest arithmetic series.

name: Bayes' Theorem
description: Updates the probability of A given observed evidence B from the reverse conditional and priors.

name: Binomial Coefficient
description: Number of ways to choose k items from n without regard to order.

name: Binomial Theorem
description: Expansion of a binomial raised to a non-negative integer power using binomial coefficients.

name: Capacitance
description: Capacitance as the ratio of stored charge to the voltage across a capacitor.

name: Centripetal Acceleration
description: Center-directed acceleration of a body in uniform circular motion.

name: Chain Rule
description: Derivative of a composition of two functions.

name: Chandrasekhar Mass
description: Maximum mass of a stable white dwarf supported by electron degeneracy pressure.

name: Combustion of Methane
description: Complete combustion of methane in oxygen, producing carbon dioxide and water.

name: Coulomb's Law
description: Electrostatic force between two point charges, inversely proportional to the square of their separation.

name: Cross Product Magnitude
description: Magnitude of the cross product of two vectors, equal to the area of the parallelogram they span.

name: de Broglie Wavelength
description: Wavelength associated with a particle of momentum p, foundational to wave–particle duality.

name: Definition of the Derivative
description: The derivative of a function as the limit of the difference quotient.

name: Determinant of a 2x2 Matrix
latex: \det\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix} = ad - bc
description: Determinant of a 2x2 matrix; nonzero iff the matrix is invertible.

name: Distance Formula
description: Straight-line distance between two points in the Cartesian plane.

name: Doppler Effect (Sound)
description: Observed frequency of a wave shifts with the relative motion of source and observer.

name: Dot Product
description: Scalar product of two vectors, equal to the product of their magnitudes and the cosine of the angle between them.

name: Eigenvalue Equation
description: Defines an eigenvector v of a matrix A with eigenvalue lambda, scaled but not rotated by A.

name: Electric Power
description: Power delivered to or dissipated by an electrical component.

name: Entropy Change (Reversible)
description: Entropy change of a system along a reversible path, integrating heat transfer over temperature.

name: Escape Velocity
description: Minimum speed needed to escape a body's gravitational field without further propulsion.

name: Euler's Formula
description: Relates the complex exponential to sine and cosine, foundational to complex analysis.

name: Euler's Identity
description: The special case of Euler's formula relating e, i, pi, 1, and 0 in a single equation.

name: Expected Value (Discrete)
description: Probability-weighted average of the values of a discrete random variable.

name: Faraday's Law of Induction
description: Induced electromotive force equals the negative rate of change of magnetic flux through a loop.

name: First Law of Thermodynamics
description: Change in a system's internal energy equals heat added to it minus work done by it.

name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
description: A definite integral equals the difference of an antiderivative F evaluated at the bounds.

name: Sum of a Geometric Series
description: Closed form for the sum of the first n terms of a geometric series with ratio r not equal to 1.

name: Gibbs Free Energy
description: Change in Gibbs free energy predicts reaction spontaneity from enthalpy, temperature, and entropy.

name: Gravitational Potential Energy (Near Earth)
description: Potential energy of a mass at height h in a uniform gravitational field near Earth's surface.

name: Heat Equation (Thermal Energy)
description: Heat required to change the temperature of a mass with specific heat capacity c.

name: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
description: A fundamental limit on the simultaneous precision of a particle's position and momentum.

name: Henderson–Hasselbalch Equation
description: Relates the pH of a buffer to the acid dissociation constant and the ratio of conjugate base to acid.

name: Hooke's Law
description: Restoring force of an ideal spring, proportional and opposite to its displacement.

name: Hubble's Law
description: The recession velocity of a distant galaxy is proportional to its distance, evidence for cosmic expansion.

name: Ideal Gas Law
description: Relates pressure, volume, temperature, and amount of an ideal gas.

name: Impulse
description: Impulse equals force times the time interval and equals the change in momentum.

name: Integration by Parts
description: Integration analogue of the product rule, used to integrate products of functions.

name: Kepler's Third Law
description: Square of an orbital period is proportional to the cube of the orbit's semi-major axis.

name: Kinematic Equation (Displacement)
aliases: s = ut + half a t squared
description: Displacement under constant acceleration from initial velocity over time t.

name: Kinematic Equation (Velocity)
description: Final velocity under constant acceleration, given initial velocity and elapsed time.

name: Kinetic Energy
description: Energy a body possesses due to its translational motion.

name: Law of Cosines
description: Relates the lengths of a triangle's sides to the cosine of one of its angles.

variables.meaning: angle between sides a and b

name: Law of Sines
description: The ratio of a triangle's side length to the sine of its opposite angle is constant.

name: Linear Momentum
description: Momentum of a body as the product of its mass and velocity.

name: Change of Base Formula
description: Converts a logarithm in one base to a ratio of logarithms in any other base k.

variables.meaning: logarithm of x in base b

name: Lorentz Factor
description: The factor by which time, length, and relativistic mass change for an object in motion.

name: Lorentz Force
description: Total electromagnetic force on a point charge moving through electric and magnetic fields.

name: Flux–Luminosity Relation
description: Observed flux of a source falls off with the inverse square of its distance from its luminosity.

name: Mass–Energy Equivalence
description: Rest energy of a body equals its mass times the speed of light squared.

name: Nernst Equation
description: Gives the potential of an electrochemical cell away from standard conditions via the reaction quotient.

name: Acid–Base Neutralization
description: A strong acid and strong base react to form a salt and water.

name: Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation
description: Attractive gravitational force between two point masses, inversely proportional to the square of their separation.

name: Newton's Second Law
description: The net force on a body equals its mass times its acceleration.

name: Normal Distribution
description: Probability density function of a normal (Gaussian) distribution with mean mu and standard deviation sigma.
relation: f = (1 / (sigma * sqrt(2 * pi))) * exp(-((x - mu)^2) / (2 * sigma^2))

name: Ohm's Law
description: Voltage across a resistor equals the current through it times its resistance.

name: pH Definition
description: The pH of a solution as the negative base-10 logarithm of the hydrogen ion activity.

name: Photon Energy
description: Energy of a photon is proportional to its frequency via the Planck constant.

name: Photosynthesis (Net Reaction)
description: Net reaction of photosynthesis: carbon dioxide and water become glucose and oxygen using light energy.

name: Product Rule
description: Derivative of the product of two functions.

name: Pythagorean Theorem
description: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the legs.

name: Pythagorean Trigonometric Identity
description: The fundamental relationship between sine and cosine of the same angle.

name: Quadratic Formula
description: Roots of a quadratic equation ax^2 + bx + c = 0 in terms of its coefficients.

name: Rotational Kinetic Energy
description: Kinetic energy of a rigid body rotating about a fixed axis, in terms of its moment of inertia and angular velocity.

name: Time-Dependent Schrödinger Equation
description: Governs the time evolution of the quantum wave function under the Hamiltonian operator.

name: Schwarzschild Radius
description: Radius of the event horizon of a non-rotating black hole of mass M.

name: Period of a Simple Pendulum
description: Period of small-amplitude oscillations of a simple pendulum of length L.

name: Snell's Law
description: Relates the angles of incidence and refraction to the refractive indices of two media.

name: Standard Deviation
description: Population standard deviation, the root-mean-square deviation from the mean.

name: Stefan–Boltzmann Law
description: Power radiated per unit area by a black body scales with the fourth power of its temperature.

name: Taylor Series
description: Represents an infinitely differentiable function as an infinite sum of terms about a point a.

name: Thin Lens Equation
description: Relates the focal length of a thin lens to the object and image distances.

name: Torque
description: Rotational analogue of force, the cross product of the lever arm and the applied force.

name: Formation of Water
description: Synthesis of water from hydrogen and oxygen gas.

name: Wave Speed
description: Speed of a wave as the product of its frequency and wavelength.

name: Wien's Displacement Law
description: The wavelength of peak emission from a black body is inversely proportional to its temperature.

name: Work Done by a Force
description: Work done by a constant force over a displacement, depending on the angle between them.
